seoTitle: How to Style Rich Text Elements in Webflow CMS
metaDescription: Customize headings, links, and lists inside a CMS-connected Rich Text block in Webflow using nested selectors.
intro: Long-form CMS content like blog posts often comes through a Rich Text element, and styling the individual tags inside it, like headings or blockquotes, requires a slightly different workflow than normal elements. This guide covers disconnecting the dynamic data temporarily, applying nested selectors, and reconnecting the field once your styles are set. It's for anyone managing a blog or article template in Webflow's CMS.

steps.heading: Locate the Rich Text element
steps.body: Open your CMS collection template page and find the dynamic Rich Text element pulling in your article content.

steps.heading: Disconnect the dynamic data temporarily
steps.body: Open the element settings panel and disconnect the CMS property so you can preview and edit static placeholder content.

steps.heading: Add a class and target child tags
steps.body: Assign a unique class to the Rich Text element, then use the nested selector option to target specific tags like h2 or blockquote inside it.

steps.heading: Adjust typography and spacing
steps.body: Set font sizes, weights, and line heights for each nested tag, and configure margin and padding for balanced paragraph spacing.

steps.heading: Reconnect the CMS field
steps.body: Once your styles look right, reopen the element settings and reconnect the Rich Text block to its original CMS collection field.

faq.question: Why do I need to disconnect the CMS data first?
faq.answer: Disconnecting lets you edit and preview static content directly inside the element, which makes it easier to select and style specific nested tags.

faq.question: Will styling changes apply to every entry in the collection?
faq.answer: Yes, since the styles are tied to the class and nested selectors on the template, they'll apply consistently across all collection items.

faq.question: What tags can I style inside a Rich Text block?
faq.answer: You can target headings, paragraphs, bulleted and numbered lists, links, and blockquotes among other standard rich text tags.

faq.question: Can I break the CMS connection permanently by mistake?
faq.answer: No, disconnecting is temporary for editing purposes, and reconnecting through the same panel restores the live dynamic data.
